OF | Zebra Technologies | | | ## 1. OVERVIEW This document is intended to provide an overview of the operation of the methods to call the Fetch. ## 2. SET UP (ADMINS ONLY) - **1.** Proceed to the Zebra Symmetry login screen. - **2.** Enter your login credentials. - **3.** Click on the main menu, and the three lines will be on the left-hand side. ![](_page_0_Picture_7.jpeg) - **4.** In the open menu list, click on the 'switch to workflows' label. - **5.** Click anywhere in the gray area to close the side bar. - **6.** Click on the 'Manage Button Groups' in the top right corner of the page. - **7.** Click on the magnifying glass to search for the desired button name. ![](_page_0_Picture_12.jpeg) | CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION | | | | | | |--------------------------|-----------|------|------|--------------|--------------------| | WORK INSTRUCTIONS | | | | | | | FETCH
OPERATION | | | | | | | Work Instruction No. | | | | | | | Revision No.
01 | Site Code | WI03 | Page | 2
4
OF | Zebra Technologies | **8.** To activate the desired button, click on the pill shape slider icon. ![](_page_1_Picture_2.jpeg) - **9.** Click the back arrow in the top left corner to return to the home page. - **10.** To use the selected button, select the button, then click on the request button that appears at the bottom of the screen. ![](_page_1_Picture_5.jpeg) ![](_page_1_Picture_6.jpeg)

01
Site Code
WI03 | Page
3
4
OF | Zebra Technologies | | # 3. SAFETY - **1.** Remember that the Robot is a Power Industrial Vehicle; therefore, you must follow the appropriate safety protocols when working around it. - A. Remove any obstruction in the work area. - B. When the Robot is in motion, give ample space even though the Robot has sensors to avoid collisions. - C. Periodically check for damage to the Robot. - **2.** Understand the Robot's capabilities and limitations. - **3.** Adapt to the Robot's pace. - **4.** For Any issues or concerns, seek the Supervisor's assistance. ## 4. INBOUND - **1.** Follow the normal receiving process. - **2.** Determine if you would be utilizing the cart as Pack1. - **3.** If using the cart, select the appropriate workflow on the Zebra tablet. - **4.** Follow the normal putaway process. - **5.** Once the putaway is complete and the cart is empty, manually move to the assigned location. ## 5. KITTING - **1.** Follow the normal Kitting process. - **2.** If the kitted order is shipping via parcel, place the product on the cart and send it to the parcel area. - **3.** Select the appropriate workflow. ## 6. PICKING - **1.** Follow the normal picking process. - **2.** When picking, decide if you will pick to the cart, a case/box, or a pallet. - **3.** If you pick to the cart, put the product on the cart and send it to the parcel area. - ✓ Ensure the product was dropped correctly to a Cone; otherwise, it will cause issues with manifesting. ## 7. PARCEL - **1.** When receiving a product on a Fetch Cart, ensure the product belongs in the parcel area. - **2.** Verify the product against the PCR.

OF | Zebra Technologies | | - **3.** Manifest the Delivery. - **4.** If unable to Manifest, seek Lead/Supervisor for assistance. - ✓ If unable to manifest, it is likely the product was not correctly dropped to a cone or not completely picked. # 8. TRASH - **1.** If using a Robot to assist with trash, ensure the CartConnect has the appropriate crate. - **2.** If the crate is full, select the appropriate workflow. - **3.** The cart will proceed to the compactors. - **4.** When done emptying the crate, ensure the crate is in its designated location. ## 9.
